# Focused on the Prize
## Scripture
"So let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up." – Galatians 6:9
## Reflection
As men, we often get caught up in the daily grind, focusing on the immediate tasks and responsibilities that demand our attention. But we must remember that our ultimate goal is not to simply get by, but to live a life that honors God and brings glory to Him. It's easy to get discouraged when we don't see immediate results, but we must keep our eyes fixed on the prize. We must stay focused on the reward that awaits us, and continue to press on, even when the going gets tough.

In 1 Corinthians 9:24-27, the Apostle Paul uses the analogy of an athlete to describe the Christian life. He reminds us that we must discipline ourselves to win the prize, to live a life that is pleasing to God. This requires focus, dedication, and perseverance. So let us not grow weary of doing good, but continue to strive for excellence in all that we do, knowing that our reward is eternal.

## Daily Challenge
"Do 10 mountain climbers while repeating the phrase 'I press on to win the prize.'"
